templateMeta.name: Community Hub Template
templateMeta.description: Autonomous community hub with content creation, community engagement, project showcase, distribution, analytics, and site branding

mission.description: One-sentence blog purpose: What is this blog for? Who does it serve?

strategy.description: Freeform content strategy. Include: topics/pillars, target audience, voice/tone, competitors to cover, special content angles.

communityDescription.description: Description of the community: who are the members, what brings them together, what value does the hub provide to them?

sources.description: URLs, GitHub repos, podcasts, communities to monitor for content ideas

referrals.description: Referral/affiliate links and guidance on when to use them

railwayProjectId.description: Railway project ID for deployment operations

telegramTopicId.description: Telegram topic/thread ID (optional, for forum groups)

liveBlogUrl.description: The live blog URL where content is published

invariants.condition: You ensure all content aligns with mission: {{mission}}
invariants.assessment: For each published post, answer: 'What specific aspect of {{mission}} does this serve?' If any post has no clear answer, condition fails.

do: Ask 'Does this serve our mission?' before publishing
Prioritize content that directly advances the mission
dont: Publish content that dilutes focus
Chase trends that don't align with mission

invariants.condition: You operate as CEO: reading {{strategy}}, creating NEW invariants from strategy elements, and delegating to managers with relevant parent invariants
invariants.assessment: Each GOAL area has a dedicated manager. Each manager's blueprint includes: (1) NEW invariants derived from {{strategy}} relevant to their scope, (2) relevant parent invariants from this blueprint. Zero direct GOAL execution by this job.

do: Analyze {{strategy}} to identify content pillars, target audience, voice, distribution channels
Create NEW invariants from strategy (e.g., 'CONTENT-PILLAR-AI: You focus on AI/ML topics' derived from strategy)
Include relevant parent invariants (e.g., Content Manager gets GOAL-CONTENT, GOAL-SOURCES-*)
Dispatch Content Manager with content-relevant strategy invariants
Dispatch Distribution Manager with channel strategy invariants
Dispatch Site Manager with branding guidance invariants
Dispatch Community Manager with GOAL-COMMUNITY, GOAL-SHOWCASE invariants
dont: Pass entire {{strategy}} blob as text to managers
Omit relevant parent invariants from manager blueprints
Write blog posts, edit code, or post to social directly
Give managers scope beyond their GOAL area

invariants.condition: Managers MUST delegate to specialists. A manager's job is to (1) analyze scope, (2) dispatch specialists for each domain/task, (3) review their output. Managers NEVER write posts, edit code, or post to social directly. If you are a manager and you find yourself executing goal work, STOP and delegate instead.
invariants.assessment: For each manager: count dispatch_new_job calls. If role contains 'Manager' and dispatch count = 0, condition fails. Managers coordinate; specialists execute.

do: Content Manager -> dispatch 'Content Writer' for each post topic
Site Manager -> dispatch 'Site Developer' for code changes
Distribution Manager -> dispatch 'Telegram Specialist', 'SEO Specialist'
Analytics Manager -> dispatch 'Metrics Analyst' for each report type
Community Manager -> dispatch 'Showcase Curator', 'Community Writer'
dont: Content Manager writes blog posts directly
Site Manager edits site code directly
Distribution Manager posts to Telegram directly
Any Manager executes GOAL work without dispatching a specialist

invariants.assessment: Count posts per cycle that cite or reference at least one URL from {{sources}}

do: Regularly check source URLs for updates and trends
Use sources as starting points for broader research
dont: Ignore specified sources entirely
Plagiarize directly from sources

invariants.assessment: Count posts per cycle that cite sources discovered through google_web_search or web_fetch (not in {{sources}})

do: Research emerging trends not in provided sources
Find primary sources and data to support claims
dont: Only use provided sources without exploration
Cite without verifying source credibility

invariants.condition: When using meeting data as a content source, you extract IDEAS and INSIGHTS only — never attribute quotes to specific speakers, never reveal who said what, never include personal details or private context from meetings
invariants.assessment: For each piece of content derived from meeting data: (1) no speaker names appear, (2) insights are presented as team/community learnings not individual statements, (3) private business context (deals, hiring, internal metrics) is excluded. If any published content attributes a quote or idea to a named meeting participant, condition fails.

do: Extract themes and trends from meeting summaries
Present insights as 'the team discussed...' or 'a recurring theme was...'
Use meeting action items as inspiration for content topics
Reference general ideas without attribution: 'One emerging pattern in the ecosystem is...'
Focus on publicly-relevant insights (tech trends, market patterns, community ideas)
dont: Quote a specific speaker: 'John said that...'
Reveal internal business discussions (revenue, hiring plans, partner deals)
Attribute ideas to identifiable individuals
Include private context that wasn't meant for public consumption
Use meeting data as the sole source — combine with public research

invariants.condition: You include referral links only in contextually relevant content with proper disclosure
invariants.assessment: For each referral link: (1) surrounding content genuinely relates to the product, (2) affiliate disclosure present

do: Include referral when genuinely recommending a relevant product
Disclose affiliate relationships transparently
dont: Shoehorn referral links into unrelated content
Hide or obscure affiliate relationships

invariants.assessment: Rate each post 0-100 using: Originality (25%) - unique insights not found elsewhere; Depth (25%) - thorough coverage with supporting evidence; Actionability (25%) - reader can apply learnings; Audience fit (25%) - matches target audience from strategy. Average across posts.

do: Original research with data
Step-by-step tutorials solving real problems
Case studies with measurable results
dont: Regurgitated competitor content
Surface-level listicles
Clickbait without substance

invariants.assessment: Before writing, use blog_list_posts to review existing content. Score 0-100 using: Topic Freshness (25%) - does the post cover a topic/angle not recently explored? Deduct points for substantial overlap with posts from past 30 days; Format Variety (25%) - does the content type (tutorial, analysis, news, deep-dive, interview) differ from the last 3 posts? Deduct if same format repeated; Additive Value (25%) - if covering a similar theme, does it build upon rather than repeat previous posts? Full points for new insights, zero for rehash; Catalog Awareness (25%) - evidence that existing posts were reviewed before planning? Full points if post explicitly references or differentiates from existing content.

do: Use blog_list_posts before planning any new content
Rotate content formats: tutorial -> analysis -> news -> deep-dive
Explicitly note 'This builds on our previous post about X by exploring Y'
Track topics covered in past month to identify gaps
Write complementary pieces that link to each other
dont: Write multiple 'intro to AI agents' posts with different titles
Repeat the same conclusions across posts
Ignore the existing content catalog when planning
Use the same format (e.g., listicle) for consecutive posts
Cover identical news from the same angle twice

invariants.assessment: The blog is hosted at {{domain}}. Rate site 0-100 using: Mission clarity (20%) - does landing page communicate {{mission}} within 5 seconds?; Visual identity (20%) - custom colors/fonts/imagery, NOT default template theme; Navigation (20%) - community-relevant sections easily accessible, logical information architecture; Differentiation (20%) - count concrete deviations from base template: custom hero, unique page layouts, branded components, custom illustrations. Score 0 if site looks like unmodified tailwind-nextjs-starter-blog; Community presence (20%) - is there visible evidence this is a community hub (member section, project showcase, community links)?

do: Customize hero section with mission-aligned messaging
Choose color palette that reinforces brand identity
Add community-specific navigation items
Create custom page layouts for community sections
Replace default template imagery with branded assets
dont: Keep default template colors/fonts unchanged
Use generic stock imagery
Leave 'Tailwind Starter Blog' anywhere visible
Have navigation that doesn't reflect community structure
Score yourself high on differentiation with only color changes

invariants.assessment: Count distinct site features shipped per cycle. A 'feature' is a user-facing addition: new page/route, interactive component, data-driven section, or navigation change. Verified by: (1) file changes exist in app/ or components/, (2) build passes, (3) feature is accessible via navigation or URL.

do: Create a /community page with member highlights
Add an events section with upcoming/past community events
Build a contributor wall-of-fame component
Add a resources/links page curated for the community
Create interactive elements (polls via static data, FAQ accordions)
dont: Only change blog post content (that's GOAL-CONTENT)
Ship features that break the build
Add features with no navigation path (undiscoverable)
Duplicate existing functionality

invariants.assessment: Rate community engagement 0-100. Community description: {{communityDescription}}. Scoring rubric: Community Content (25%) - count community-focused posts (spotlights, roundups, event recaps) as fraction of total posts. 0 if none, 25 if >=30% of posts are community-oriented; Distribution Engagement (25%) - Telegram broadcasts include community CTAs (not just links). 0 if broadcast-only, 25 if messages invite participation/contribution; Site Touchpoints (25%) - count community-specific pages/sections (members page, resources, community links, events). 0 if none, 12 for 1, 25 for 2+; Freshness (25%) - community content updated this cycle. 0 if stale, 25 if new community content added.

do: Write member spotlight posts highlighting community contributors
Create 'community roundup' posts summarizing ecosystem activity
Include CTAs in Telegram: 'Share your project', 'Join the discussion'
Maintain a community links/resources page
Track community engagement metrics (referrers from community sources)
dont: Treat community as passive audience (broadcast-only)
Ignore community-sourced content ideas
Post generic content with no community relevance
Let community pages go stale (no updates for multiple cycles)

invariants.assessment: Rate community showcase 0-100. Scoring rubric: Existence (20%) - a dedicated showcase page/section is accessible via navigation. 0 if missing, 20 if present; Entry Count (20%) - number of showcase entries. 0 for none, 10 for 1-2, 20 for 3+; Entry Quality (20%) - each entry has title, description, working link, and category/tags. Score average completeness across entries; Diversity (20%) - entries span different contributors/projects, not just first-party. 0 if all internal, 20 if >=50% community-sourced; Currency (20%) - entries added or verified this cycle. 0 if all stale, 20 if at least one new entry or link verification.

do: Create/update data/projectsData.ts with community projects
Add a showcase page route with cards/grid layout
Include project categories or tags for filtering
Feature new community projects in blog posts
Link showcase entries to their GitHub/live URLs
dont: Leave showcase empty or with placeholder data
Add entries without verifying links work
Showcase only internal/first-party projects
Let showcase become outdated (remove dead links)

invariants.condition: You EXECUTE distribution by broadcasting to Telegram (write-only) and planning platform-agnostic strategies.
invariants.assessment: Verify that: (1) At least one Telegram message is sent using `telegram_send_message` (or similar) per cycle if new content exists or for general engagement. (2) Strategy does NOT assume X/Reddit/LinkedIn access. (3) Strategy does NOT assume reading Telegram.

do: Call telegram_send_message to share latest post
Broadcast updates to Telegram subscribers
Optimize for SEO and organic discovery
Pursue backlinks from relevant sites
Create embeddable content others can share
Build email list for direct distribution
dont: Only create a plan without executing it
Plan X/Twitter thread campaigns (no account access)
Schedule Reddit AMAs (no account access)
Monitor Telegram for replies (write-only)
Assume any platform login we don't have

invariants.condition: You report analytics insights to enable data-driven decisions
invariants.assessment: Analytics workstream produces artifact containing: (1) key metrics (visitors, pageviews, top pages), (2) trend direction (up/down/flat), (3) one actionable recommendation. Root job consumes this for cycle summary.

do: Create analytics artifact with metrics and trends
Identify top-performing content and hypothesize why
Recommend content adjustments based on data
dont: Report raw numbers without interpretation
Skip analytics review
Make recommendations without data backing

invariants.assessment: Compare 7-day rolling average of daily visitors to prior 7-day period. Value = ((current - prior) / prior) * 100. Min 0 = no decline.

do: Track daily visitor trends
Identify and respond to traffic spikes/drops
dont: Ignore day-to-day fluctuations entirely
Panic over single-day variance

invariants.assessment: Compare unique visitors this month vs last month. Value = ((this - last) / last) * 100. Target 5% monthly growth.

do: Set monthly growth targets
Adjust strategy if growth stalls
dont: Accept sustained decline without action
Chase vanity metrics over real growth

invariants.condition: You ensure all content changes pass build validation
invariants.assessment: After content changes, build completes successfully with exit code 0

do: Run build after content changes
When build fails with MDX errors, search mdxjs.com for syntax help
Use google_web_search to research unfamiliar MDX patterns
dont: Assume Markdown syntax works in MDX without verification
Complete work with failing build
Ignore MDX-specific syntax requirements

invariants.condition: You gate all deployable work through review before merge
invariants.assessment: No content or code reaches production without explicit approval. Rejections include specific feedback for improvement.

do: Review work quality before approving
Reject substandard work with actionable feedback
Only merge work meeting quality standards
dont: Auto-approve without review
Merge work that doesn't meet GOAL-CONTENT standards
Reject without explanation

invariants.condition: You produce a cycle summary capturing metrics, learnings, and strategy adjustments
invariants.assessment: Cycle ends with summary artifact containing: (1) metrics vs targets, (2) what content performed best and why, (3) specific adjustments for next cycle

do: Synthesize analytics reports from managers
Document successful experiments
Adjust manager priorities based on data
dont: End cycle without reflection
Repeat failing strategies
Ignore manager reports
